symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations, chest tightness and dyspnoea at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergic cardiorespiratory symptoms are typical of panic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-exertional, non-radiating chest tightness at rest after a trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest onset after a psychological trigger without exertional/radiating features lowers ACS probability, though it cannot fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pleuritic pain, DVT features or immobility

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low PE risk factors and normal SpO2 lower PE probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperventilation with perioral/finger paraesthesiae; prior panic episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Respiratory-alkalosis paraesthesiae with prior identical episodes strongly support panic.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic severe anaemia / occult GI bleeding
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No bleeding or postural symptoms argues against symptomatic severe anaemia/acute blood loss.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient iron studies/FBC and coeliac gastroenterology pathway
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Manage the coeliac serology and mild iron deficiency electively via GP; not urgent.
Not required remotely in a clearly low-risk panic attack with negative cardiac screen; would be arranged only if cardiac red flags were present.
